title: Thread-Safe TTL Cache
description: Make an existing TTL cache implementation thread-safe, enforce TTL expiry on reads, and fix planted bugs in eviction and edge-case handling. Get/put must remain amortised O(1).
starter_code_note: The data-structure choice (collections.OrderedDict with move_to_end-based promotion) is provided in the starter code. The candidate is expected to add synchronisation, fix the off-by-one in the eviction loop, handle capacity=0, and enforce TTL on get(). Do NOT credit them for the algorithm or data-structure choice.
code_quality_criteria: Correctness (does it pass the tests and fix the planted traps?)
Thread safety (lock placement, no data races on OrderedDict)
Idiomatic Python (typing, context managers, time.monotonic for TTL)
architectural_criteria: Lock placement and granularity (per-method, scope of critical section)
Deadlock avoidance (no nested locking, careful with reentrant calls)
Edge case handling (capacity=0 no-op, eviction boundary off-by-one, TTL on get)
Monotonic time source (time.monotonic vs time.time for TTL comparisons)

tasks.description: Core get/put and LRU eviction behaviour.
Concurrent get/put do not corrupt cache state.
Boundary handling such as capacity=0 and unhashable keys.
Expired entries are not returned from get().
